EQT.name: EQT Partners
EQT.description: EQT Partners is the adviser to EQT AB, a Swedish global investment organisation founded in 1994 and listed on Nasdaq Stockholm. Its funds invest across private equity—including large-cap and mid-market buyouts, growth equity, venture, healthcare, and life sciences—as well as real assets such as infrastructure and real estate, credit, and private wealth strategies, with geographic reach in Europe, North America, and Asia-Pacific. As of the mid-2020s the group manages on the order of EUR 270 billion in assets under management, with a substantial share fee-generating, and oversees a portfolio of more than three hundred companies alongside a growing secondaries and GP-stakes capability highlighted by the announced acquisition of Coller Capital. EQT routinely ranks among the world’s largest private markets firms by capital raised and presents itself as a purpose-driven, thematic investor with deep Nordic roots and a fully global footprint.

Nordic Capital.name: Nordic Capital
Nordic Capital.description: Nordic Capital is a global private equity firm that specialises in leveraged buyouts and significant minority investments in established, market-leading companies in Northern Europe and North America. Founded in 1989 and headquartered in Stockholm, it manages on the order of EUR 34 billion in committed capital and maintains offices in Copenhagen, Frankfurt, Helsinki, Jersey, London, Luxembourg, New York, Oslo, and Seoul—reflecting both its Nordic roots and its transatlantic deal flow. The firm concentrates on four industry verticals: healthcare; technology and payments; financial services; and a broad bucket of services and industrial technology where operational improvement and thematic insight matter as much as financial structuring. Nordic Capital has repeatedly appeared among the twenty-five largest private equity firms globally in industry rankings, underpinned by large flagship funds and parallel Evolution vehicles that extend its capacity to hold and scale complex assets.

Triton.name: Triton
Triton.description: Triton is a European private equity firm founded in 1997 and headquartered in London, with investment hubs in Frankfurt, Luxembourg, Stockholm, and Jersey. It presents itself as an operationally minded mid-market buyer in business services, industrial technology, and healthcare, with a trademark slogan—“Buy Well, Build Better, Sell Strong”—that captures its emphasis on due diligence, post-close transformation, and exit timing. Triton has raised more than EUR 21 billion across successive fund generations—including large flagship buyout funds, a smaller mid-cap programme, and debt opportunities vehicles—and counts more than one hundred primary investments and many hundreds of platform and add-on transactions. An internal Accelerator Unit supports portfolio executives on commercial, digital, and ESG priorities.

Amplio.name: Amplio
Amplio.description: Amplio is a Swedish private equity firm established in 2024 by the team that previously built Segulah’s franchise. It focuses on the Nordic lower mid-market—enterprise values typically between SEK 100 million and SEK 500 million—where it can be the first institutional owner and professionalise founder-led businesses in business services and IT and technology services. Amplio’s mandate is explicitly tied to sustainability themes such as sustainable solutions, digital efficiency, and smarter cities, and its flagship vehicle is classified under SFDR Article 8. The same team continues to manage legacy Segulah V capital while deploying Amplio I, drawing on more than a decade of joint experience and a long record of add-on acquisitions across the region.

Altor.name: Altor Equity Partners
Altor.description: Altor Equity Partners is a Nordic private equity firm founded in 2003 and headquartered in Stockholm, with additional offices in Copenhagen, Oslo, Helsinki, and Zurich. It describes its work as transformational private equity: majority investments, often in the upper mid-market across the Nordics and DACH, where operational change, digitalisation, and thematic work on energy and the green transition can move the needle. Over two decades Altor has invested in close to a hundred companies and completed more than three hundred add-on acquisitions, supported by a team of well over a hundred professionals. Recent fund generations have moved toward longer fifteen-year horizons and an explicitly ESG-integrated process, including partnerships such as WovenLight on AI-enabled value creation. Consumer, healthcare, industrials, financial services, and technology remain the primary sector lanes.

fundraising.current_fund: Altor VI (>€3.0bn, closed Dec 2023); seven funds, >€12bn total committed
fundraising.strategy: Upper mid-cap transformational PE; Nordic + DACH; 15-year fund horizon; ESG-integrated; WovenLight AI partnership

Litorina.name: Litorina
Litorina.description: Litorina is a Swedish private equity firm founded in 1998 that has spent more than twenty-five years investing in profitable Nordic companies that lead narrow product or service niches with sales typically between EUR 10 million and EUR 100 million. The team brings over a century of combined transaction experience and has completed more than fifty investments, with around a dozen active portfolio companies at any given time. Litorina’s value-creation playbook emphasises consolidation, geographic roll-out, and international expansion—often through systematic buy-and-build—in both business services and consumer-facing businesses. The firm has also explored continuation structures to align timelines with long-duration niche assets.

fundraising.current_fund: Litorina V (SEK 3bn latest fund)
fundraising.next_fund: Continuation fund seeking (2025) to reset valuations for select holdings
fundraising.strategy: Nordic niche market leaders; EUR 10–100M sales; business services and consumer; buy-and-build; Litorina playbook

Adelis Equity.name: Adelis Equity Partners
Adelis Equity.description: Adelis Equity Partners is a growth-oriented private equity firm anchored in Stockholm with teams in Oslo, Helsinki, Copenhagen, and Munich. Founded in 2012, it backs small and medium-sized businesses across Northern Europe—typically with revenues in the tens to low hundreds of millions of euros—where founders or families are ready for a professional majority partner. Healthcare and life science, technology and software, and business services form the backbone of the strategy, and the firm is known for energetic buy-and-build execution: roughly fifty platform investments and well over two hundred add-ons since inception. Adelis has raised more than EUR 4.25 billion to date, including a EUR 1.6 billion fourth fund that closed in 2025; recent platforms include Cedra (Nordic audit/advisory), Circura (building renewal and rehabilitation), and defence technology alongside software leaders such as Bryntum. It remains focused on control-oriented growth buyouts rather than passive financial engineering.

fundraising.current_fund: Fund IV (€1.6bn, closed March 2025); Fund III (€932m, 2021); total raised >€4.25bn
fundraising.strategy: Growth buyouts in €10–300M revenue companies; Nordic + Germany; Healthcare & Life Science, Tech & Software, Business Services; Munich office (2023)

Ratos.name: Ratos
Ratos.description: Ratos is a Swedish investment company listed on Nasdaq Stockholm with corporate origins in 1866, making it one of the Nordic region’s oldest continuously operating industrial and investment names. Today it behaves as an active owner of small and mid-sized unlisted companies—currently fourteen portfolio businesses with combined net sales around SEK 19 billion and roughly nine thousand employees—clustered in industry, construction and services, and consumer end-markets. Ratos emphasises subsidiary autonomy, board-level governance, and sustainability integration without imposing a single operating model on diverse businesses. The listed structure gives permanent capital and transparent reporting, while adjusted EBITA and cash conversion remain central measures of whether ownership is working.

Summa Equity.name: Summa Equity
Summa Equity.description: Summa Equity is a Stockholm-based thematic impact investor founded in 2016 that deploys private equity and growth capital only where it sees a credible link between financial return and measurable progress on global sustainability challenges. The firm organises its work around themes such as circular economy, sustainable food systems, energy transition, tech-enabled resilience, and changing demographics, and it manages roughly EUR 4 billion across more than thirty-five investments in Europe and North America. Summa III, closed at about EUR 2.3 billion, was marketed as one of Europe’s largest Article 9 impact funds at the time of fundraising. Portfolio examples such as Oda, Nofitech, Tibber, and NG Nordic illustrate how Summa combines deep sector work with the Summa Foundation’s broader philanthropic ambitions.

Accent Equity.name: Accent Equity Partners
Accent Equity.description: Accent Equity Partners is a Stockholm-based investor that has backed private Nordic companies since 1994. The firm works as a lead or co-lead partner in buyouts and expansion capital, typically in established businesses where EBITA and equity cheques fit its lower- to mid-market sweet spot and where industrial services, niche manufacturing, and broader business services have long been a natural hunting ground. Across seven funds it has built a diversified Nordic portfolio—Tempcon, Enerco, and Triarca are among the better-known industrial names—with total commitments well above EUR 1.5 billion. Accent Equity VII closed in 2025 and a SEK 1.5 billion continuation vehicle (Accent Equity CV) in 2026; the team continues to focus on companies that already have a proven, scalable model and room to grow across the region.

IK Partners.name: IK Partners
IK Partners.description: IK Partners is a European mid-market private equity firm headquartered in London with roots dating to 1989. It has raised more than EUR 20 billion across seventeen fund generations and invested in over two hundred companies, describing its culture as people-first and its approach as partnership-driven rather than transaction-led. Geographically IK focuses on Benelux, the DACH region, France, the Nordics, and the United Kingdom, with four complementary strategies: Mid Cap for larger buyouts, Small Cap for companies up to roughly EUR 200 million enterprise value, Development Capital for smaller growth situations, and a Partnership Fund for significant minority stakes in larger businesses. Platform investments are typically complemented by active add-on programmes across business services, healthcare, and industrials.

Verdane.name: Verdane
Verdane.description: Verdane is a European growth equity investor headquartered in Oslo with offices across the Nordics, Germany, and London. Founded in 2003, it has raised roughly EUR 9 billion and completed more than two hundred investments, concentrating on equity tickets between EUR 20 million and EUR 150 million in software, digital consumer services, and sustainability-related technology. The platform is organised into parallel strategies—Edda for software consolidation, Freya for flexible growth and buyouts, Idun as an Article 9 impact fund, and Capital for broader tech growth—so that different risk-return and ESG profiles can sit under one partnership. Verdane has also pledged to neutralise its scope one through three emissions using engineered carbon removal, signalling how seriously it treats climate risk alongside digitalisation and decarbonisation as core investment themes.

Valedo Partners.name: Valedo Partners
Valedo Partners.description: Valedo Partners is a Stockholm-based growth-oriented private equity firm founded in 2006 by professionals who spun out of EQT, carrying forward a culture of active ownership but with a deliberate focus on founder- and family-led businesses. It targets established, profitable Nordic companies with sales typically between SEK 100 million and SEK 500 million, and in roughly eighty-five percent of cases it invests alongside previous owners rather than replacing them on day one. Valedo has backed more than thirty-five platforms, completed on the order of three hundred and fifty add-ons, and reports that realised investments grew revenue about threefold on average during ownership—evidence of a buy-and-build and operational playbook applied with patience. The firm intends to deploy around SEK 2 billion over a three- to five-year window in its current vintage.

Alder.name: Alder
Alder.description: Alder is a Stockholm-based private equity firm that invests exclusively in sustainable technology—industrial tech, clean tech, and related businesses that combine commercial upside with measurable environmental benefit. Since its founding in 2008 it has concentrated on Nordic (and selectively broader European) companies with turnover roughly between SEK 100 million and SEK 750 million, where product and market fit are already visible and capital can accelerate scale. The firm’s third fund, Alder III, closed in May 2024 at SEK 3.1 billion and is structured as an SFDR Article 9 “dark green” vehicle, reflecting an investment mandate that treats sustainability as core to the thesis rather than an overlay. Alder’s story is one of deep sector conviction and long holding periods alongside entrepreneurs who are building the low-carbon and resource-efficient economy.

Bure Equity.name: Bure Equity
Bure Equity.description: Bure Equity is a Swedish listed investment company on Nasdaq Stockholm that combines the permanence of balance-sheet capital with an active-ownership mindset in both listed and unlisted holdings. Tracing its corporate roots to 1992, Bure today mixes significant stakes in listed names such as Mycronic, Vitrolife, and Yubico with a portfolio of unlisted growth and technology businesses—including Atle, Mentimeter, Mercuri International, and Silex Microsystems—across healthcare, technology, and broader STEM-related themes. Sustainability is integrated into how the board and management engage with portfolio companies rather than treated as a separate reporting exercise. The model is deliberately long-term: Bure can hold positions for many years when the industrial logic warrants it.

CapMan.name: CapMan
CapMan.description: CapMan is a Helsinki-based private asset manager listed on Nasdaq Helsinki and active across the Nordic region and wider Europe since 1989. What began as a classic Nordic buyout franchise has evolved into a multi-strategy platform that spans private equity (buyout, growth, and special situations), real estate, infrastructure, natural capital, and real-asset debt, serving a broad base of institutional clients. The firm manages on the order of EUR 7 billion in assets under management—part of more than EUR 8 billion raised since inception—and employs more than two hundred professionals from offices in Helsinki, Stockholm, Copenhagen, Oslo, London, Luxembourg, and Düsseldorf. CapMan emphasises responsible investment across all asset classes and has sought third-party validation of its sustainability approach, including PRI assessments that cover its main investment verticals.

Celero.name: Celero Capital
Celero.description: Celero Capital is a Nordic lower mid-market private equity firm founded in 2022 in Stockholm, with a Norwegian presence in Oslo, by a team led by Peter Møller with prior experience at FSN Capital, Permira, and Goldman Sachs. The firm targets equity tickets roughly between EUR 20 million and EUR 150 million in services, consumer goods, specialised industrials, property-related services, and manufacturing solutions—segments where hands-on operational support can unlock scale. Celero applies a proprietary set of “Accelerator Modules” to portfolio companies, works as a UNPRI signatory with an SFDR Article 8 fund, and secured cornerstone backing from the European Investment Fund under InvestEU for its first institutional vehicle, which closed above EUR 300 million in 2024.

Polaris.name: Polaris Private Equity
Polaris.description: Polaris Private Equity is a Copenhagen-based mid-market firm founded in 1998 with an additional office in Oslo and a reputation for deep work in industrial and business-services companies across the Nordics. It has completed more than fifty investments and typically backs businesses with equity tickets in the EUR 20–80 million range and sales roughly between EUR 20 million and EUR 300 million. Recent funds—including Polaris Fund V and the Flexible Capital sleeve—have raised more than DKK 11 billion in aggregate, allowing the firm to support both traditional five- to seven-year holds and longer-duration industrial themes such as decarbonisation and energy efficiency (illustrated by holdings like Micropower, LEAB, and Vinnergi). Polaris combines sector specialisation with a flexible attitude to ownership length when the industrial logic demands patience.

portfolio_companies.name: Salfarm
portfolio_companies.detailed_description: Salfarm is a Nordic animal pharma company headquartered in Kolding, Denmark, specializing in veterinary pharmaceuticals and animal health products for livestock and companion animals. Polaris Private Equity entered a partnership with Salfarm and FaunaPharma in November 2024 to support growth in the animal health sector. The company employs approximately 30 people and serves the Nordic livestock and companion animal markets. The investment aligns with Polaris's focus on healthcare and life sciences, targeting the growing demand for animal health solutions driven by protein consumption trends, pet ownership growth, and regulatory standards for livestock care.

portfolio_companies.name: 7N
portfolio_companies.detailed_description: 7N is a premium Danish freelancer IT consultancy headquartered in Copenhagen, with revenue of approximately €210 million and 220+ employees. Polaris entered a partnership with 7N in October 2024. In July 2025, Polaris created a powerful strategic IT partner through the acquisition of Globeteam and merger with 7N, forming a combined Nordic IT services platform. The merged entity provides high-end IT consulting and freelancer solutions to enterprises across Denmark, Sweden, and the Nordics. The investment supports Polaris's thesis on tech-enabled business services and the growing demand for flexible, specialist IT talent.

portfolio_companies.name: Vinnergi
portfolio_companies.detailed_description: Vinnergi is a Swedish technical engineering consultancy headquartered in Linköping, with revenue of approximately €66 million and 550 employees. The company provides specialized engineering services across telecom, energy, and buildings sectors. Polaris entered a partnership with Vinnergi in April 2024 as part of its industrial decarbonisation theme alongside Micropower Group and LEAB.

portfolio_companies.name: Awardit
portfolio_companies.description: Customer clubs and loyalty programs operator
portfolio_companies.detailed_description: Awardit is a Swedish provider of customer clubs, loyalty programs, and incentive solutions. Polaris invested in December 2023 and announced a public offer in March 2024; Awardit was taken private by Polaris in May 2025. In August 2025, Awardit entered a partnership with German incentive and loyalty specialist Buben & Mädchen. The company has revenue of approximately €91 million and 256 employees, serving B2B clients across the Nordics and Europe.

portfolio_companies.name: Micropower Group
portfolio_companies.detailed_description: Micropower Group is a Swedish manufacturer of batteries and chargers for industrial, material handling, and mobility applications. Polaris entered a partnership in May 2023. In June 2025, LEAB became part of Micropower Group, strengthening Polaris's industrial decarbonisation theme. The company is headquartered in Växjö with revenue of approximately €130 million and 460 employees.

portfolio_companies.name: Scanmetals
portfolio_companies.detailed_description: Scanmetals is a Danish specialist metals recycling company recovering metallic waste from incineration plants, with facilities in Denmark and the United Kingdom. Polaris invested in 2025. In May 2025, Lars Fuglsang was appointed CEO with founder Ejvind Pedersen transitioning to the board; the company refinanced and upsized credit facilities and was re-certified for End-of-Waste status. Revenue approximately €147 million, 200 employees.

portfolio_companies.name: Hoyer
portfolio_companies.description: Manufacturer of electric motors, drives, and controls
portfolio_companies.detailed_description: Hoyer is a Danish manufacturer of electric motors, drives, and controls headquartered in Hadsten. Majority owner is Capidea (Danish PE); Polaris and Motortramp are minority co-investors alongside Capidea — not a Polaris-controlled buyout. Revenue approximately €98 million and 250 employees.

Nordstjernan.name: Nordstjernan
Nordstjernan.description: Nordstjernan is a Stockholm-based investment house owned by the Axel and Margaret Ax:son Johnson foundations, with a history stretching to 1890 and a formal investment-company structure since 1999. Its mandate is unusual in Nordic private markets: safeguard the foundations’ long-term capital while acting as a responsible steward of industrial and commercial heritage. The portfolio—on the order of SEK 50 billion—mixes long-held operating companies with more recent growth and technology positions, and Nordstjernan has invested in more than forty companies over twenty-five years while realising over thirty exits. The organisation can remain invested for decades when the strategic rationale warrants patience, and it emphasises strong risk-adjusted returns alongside governance that aligns with foundation ownership.

fundraising.current_fund: Permanent capital (foundation-owned)
fundraising.strategy: Active, responsible ownership; strong risk-adjusted returns; may invest for limited time or decades

portfolio_companies.name: Aidian
portfolio_companies.detailed_description: Aidian is a Finnish diagnostics company developing, manufacturing, and selling instruments and tests for healthcare. Nordstjernan acquired the company in March 2022. Ferd acquired 33% of Aidian in May 2022. The company focuses on point-of-care and laboratory diagnostics with applications across clinical and veterinary markets.

portfolio_companies.name: Dacke Industri
portfolio_companies.description: Technical industrial companies with proprietary products
portfolio_companies.detailed_description: Dacke Industri is a Swedish developer and investor in technical industrial companies with proprietary products and customer-specific components. Nordstjernan has been a long-term owner since 2016. The group builds and operates niche industrial businesses with strong technical capabilities, focusing on firms with proprietary technology and defensible market positions. In 2024, Dacke was among Nordstjernan's most active companies for add-on acquisitions, contributing to the group's 42 add-on investments that year. The portfolio spans industrial manufacturing, components, and technical solutions across the Nordics.

portfolio_companies.name: Etac
portfolio_companies.description: World-leading assistive devices and patient transfer products
portfolio_companies.detailed_description: Etac is a world-leading Swedish supplier of assistive devices for people with disabilities and products for patient transfers. Nordstjernan has held the company since 2007. Etac offers wheelchairs, walking aids, bathing and toileting solutions, and patient handling equipment. In 2022, Etac invested in Ki Mobility (US) to expand its mobility offerings. The company serves healthcare providers, care homes, and individuals globally. Etac was among Nordstjernan's most acquisition-active portfolio companies in 2024. The investment thesis centers on demographic trends (aging population), regulatory support for accessibility, and the company's strong brand and distribution in the Nordic and global welfare technology markets.

portfolio_companies.name: Prima Vård
portfolio_companies.description: Primary care, women's and children's health, mental health
portfolio_companies.detailed_description: Prima Vård is a Swedish healthcare company operating within primary care, women's and children's health, mental health, and occupational health. Nordstjernan has held the company since 2017. The group operates clinics and care units across Sweden, serving both public and private healthcare segments.

portfolio_companies.name: Rosti
portfolio_companies.description: Global contract manufacturer in plastic injection molding
portfolio_companies.detailed_description: Rosti is a global Danish contract manufacturer specializing in plastic injection molding with over 80 years of experience. Nordstjernan has been a long-term owner since 2004. In 2022, Rosti acquired Plastic Components, Inc. (US). The company serves healthcare, consumer, and industrial sectors. Nordstjernan divested Rosti Automotive in 2020.

portfolio_companies.name: Sunparadise
portfolio_companies.description: Glazing products for homes, balconies, and facades
portfolio_companies.detailed_description: Sunparadise is a Swedish developer and manufacturer of glazing products for homes, balconies, and facades. Nordstjernan has held the company since 2009. The company is active in the DACH region, Scandinavia, and wider Europe, offering solutions that extend living space and improve energy efficiency.

portfolio_companies.name: Gnothis
portfolio_companies.detailed_description: Gnothis is a Swedish med-tech company within Nordstjernan's healthcare portfolio. Nordstjernan invested in 2022. The company operates in specialized medical technology with limited public information available.

portfolio_companies.name: Alligo
portfolio_companies.description: Leading Nordic specialist in workwear, PPE, tools, and supplies
portfolio_companies.detailed_description: Alligo is the leading Nordic specialist in workwear, personal protective equipment (PPE), tools, and supplies for professional and industrial customers. Nordstjernan has been a major shareholder since 2015. The company serves construction, industry, healthcare, and public sector clients across Sweden, Norway, Finland, and Denmark through a combination of physical stores, e-commerce, and B2B sales. Alligo was among Nordstjernan's most active companies for add-on acquisitions in 2024. Nordstjernan has periodically issued call options in Alligo (2022, 2025) as part of its capital structure management. The company benefits from workplace safety regulation, professionalization of trades, and consolidation in the fragmented Nordic workwear and tools market.

portfolio_companies.name: Attendo
portfolio_companies.detailed_description: Attendo is the Nordic region's largest care company with 700+ operations and 30,000 employees across Sweden, Denmark, and Finland. Nordstjernan has been a major shareholder since 2015. The company provides elderly care, disability care, and mental health services in both publicly financed and private segments.

portfolio_companies.name: Bonava
portfolio_companies.detailed_description: Bonava is one of Northern Europe's largest housing developers with approximately 8,000 homes in production. Nordstjernan has been a shareholder since 2016. The company develops residential projects across Sweden, Germany, Finland, Estonia, Latvia, and Lithuania. Nordstjernan has issued call options in Bonava (2020) as part of capital structure management.

portfolio_companies.name: Momentum Group
portfolio_companies.description: Leading Nordic supplier of industrial components and services
portfolio_companies.detailed_description: Momentum Group is a group of companies forming a leading Nordic supplier of industrial components and related services. Nordstjernan made a mandatory public offer in November 2020 and acquired additional shares. Nordstjernan has sold down shares in Momentum (2023) while retaining significant ownership. The company serves industrial customers across the Nordics through its network of subsidiaries.

portfolio_companies.name: Nobia
portfolio_companies.description: One of Europe's largest kitchen specialists
portfolio_companies.detailed_description: Nobia is one of Europe's largest kitchen specialists, designing, manufacturing, and selling kitchens under brands including Marbodal, Magnet, Älvsjö, and HTH. Nordstjernan has been a shareholder since 2010. The company operates across the Nordics and parts of Europe. Nordstjernan has issued call options in Nobia (2020).

portfolio_companies.name: Circura Group
portfolio_companies.description: Nordic platform for building renewal services
portfolio_companies.detailed_description: Circura Group is a newly established Nordic platform for building renewal services, targeting SEK 20 billion in revenue by 2030. The platform combines expertise in facade renovation, roofing, and building maintenance. Nordstjernan invested in 2025.

Nalka.name: Nalka
Nalka.aum: Undisclosed (12 portfolio companies, 7,000+ employees, 65+ add-ons since 2020)
Nalka.description: Nalka is an investor in market-leading small and medium-sized enterprises in the Nordic countries and the DACH region, backed by permanent capital from Interogo Holding rather than a conventional ten-year fund structure. Headquartered in Stockholm with a Munich office, it develops independent, competitive businesses that management and Nalka believe can remain profitable and sustainable over decades. The portfolio comprises roughly a dozen platform companies employing more than seven thousand people, and since 2020 the group has executed more than sixty-five add-on acquisitions—evidence of an active buy-and-build style within industrial, business services, technology, logistics, and consumer end-markets. The Interogo link to the IKEA ecosystem informs a long-term, values-conscious ownership philosophy even though portfolio companies operate on a fully arm’s-length basis.

fundraising.current_fund: Permanent capital via Interogo Holding
fundraising.strategy: No traditional fund structure; invests via balance sheet of Interogo Holding

Impilo.name: Impilo
Impilo.description: Impilo is a Stockholm-based investment firm devoted entirely to healthcare—pharmaceuticals, medtech, specialist pharma services, and adjacent care delivery—across the Nordic region. Founded in 2018, it pursues majority positions in businesses with enterprise values typically between EUR 50 million and EUR 200 million, though it can also take meaningful minorities when the strategic fit is strong. Impilo articulates its mission through the “Health Diamond”: improving quality of care, increasing availability, lowering system cost, and preventing avoidable care where possible. Its second fund reached a EUR 700 million hard cap in 2025, and the firm pairs commercial ambition with a published sustainability handbook and UNPRI-aligned reporting so that health impact and ESG considerations stay embedded in ownership.

Axcel.name: Axcel
Axcel.description: Axcel is a Copenhagen-headquartered private equity firm that has invested in the Nordic and wider Northern European mid-market since 1994, with additional investment presence in Stockholm and Frankfurt. Over nine fund generations it has raised more than EUR 4.8 billion in committed capital and built a track record that spans dozens of platform companies, several hundred bolt-on transactions, and a large number of realisations—work that reflects a consistent emphasis on commercial excellence, digitalisation, buy-and-build, and sustainability as levers of value. The firm organises its deal flow around four sectors: technology; business services and industrials; healthcare; and consumer. Axcel’s Elevate strategy and continuation vehicles sit alongside its main fund line, and Fund VIII was in the market with a target above DKK 10 billion as the firm entered the second half of the 2020s.

MVI.name: MVI
MVI.description: MVI is a Stockholm-based private equity investor focused on profitable Nordic SMEs with turnover generally between SEK 50 million and SEK 250 million and clear structural growth ahead. Founded in 1992, the firm organises its sourcing around three long-term themes—digitalisation, circular economy, and broader societal transformation—and has supported more than twenty portfolio companies with the help of a network of roughly fifty dedicated professionals and advisers. MVI presents itself as entrepreneur-centric: fewer, deeper relationships, emphasis on trust and shared ambition, and a refusal to treat portfolio companies as interchangeable financial assets. Successive funds since 2016 have refined the same Nordic mid-market buyout formula.

Equip.name: Equip Capital
Equip.description: Equip Capital is a Norwegian private equity firm founded in 2018 by five partners with more than a century of combined experience in Nordic investing. Based in Oslo, it concentrates on small and mid-sized companies across consumer, industrials, business services, technology, and leisure where a control-oriented owner can support the next stage of profitable growth. Equip has raised several successive vehicles—including Fund I at roughly EUR 180 million, Fund II near EUR 285 million, and a larger single-purpose commitment around EUR 306 million—backed by institutional Nordic and international LPs. The portfolio now counts nearly twenty companies and the firm is an active member of the Norwegian Venture Capital and Private Equity Association.

Trill Impact.name: Trill Impact
Trill Impact.description: Trill Impact is a Swedish private markets firm founded in 2019 that invests exclusively for impact—no parallel non-impact mandates—across buyout, venture, and microfinance strategies. The buyout sleeve targets mid-market companies in Northern Europe where environmental or social outcomes can be verified alongside cash flows; ventures back early-stage businesses in climate and health; and microfinance provides responsible credit in emerging markets. Trill submits to independent BlueMark verification of alignment with the Operating Principles for Impact Management and has received top-tier PRI scores, reflecting institutional investors’ demand for discipline around impact claims. Thematically the firm groups its work under sustainable planet, resilient society, and healthy people.

Norvestor.name: Norvestor
Norvestor.description: Norvestor is a Nordic private equity firm founded in Oslo in 1989 that focuses on the lower mid-market, where it can apply what it calls “industrialised entrepreneurship”—systematic buy-and-build, operational cadence, and founder-friendly transitions. The firm has completed more than eighty platform investments and maintains roughly twenty-five active holdings, with successive funds scaling from Norvestor IX at EUR 1.5 billion to the Nova programme for slightly smaller situations and Norvestor X opening to investors in 2025. Sector emphasis falls on business services, tech-enabled services, consumer services, and industrial solutions, often with a path from Nordic domestic champions to pan-European platforms. Norvestor has also embraced GP-led continuation vehicles to align duration with value creation when public markets or strategic buyers are not the right exit.

Helix Kapital.name: Helix Kapital
Helix Kapital.description: Helix Kapital is a Stockholm-based growth-oriented private equity firm established in 2008 that partners with entrepreneurial management teams to accelerate already profitable businesses in Sweden and the wider Nordics. Its sweet spot is tech-enabled B2B services, industrial technology, and software that modernises traditional industries—businesses where buy-and-build and operational discipline can compound returns. Helix Kapital Fund III closed in April 2024 at SEK 1.566 billion, was heavily oversubscribed, carries an SFDR Article 8 classification, and includes backing from the InvestEU programme, underscoring both institutional demand for the strategy and the firm’s emphasis on responsible investment alongside financial outcomes.

FSN Capital.name: FSN Capital
FSN Capital.description: FSN Capital is a Northern European private equity firm founded in Oslo in 1999 and recognised for mid-cap transformational ownership rather than passive financial sponsorship. It typically invests in companies with enterprise values between roughly EUR 100 million and EUR 1 billion, bridging the Nordics with continental Europe and backing platforms in services, industrials, healthcare, technology, and consumer. The firm’s public narrative stresses a purpose—“contributing to the welfare of everyday people”—expressed through operational improvement and long holding periods where appropriate. Across six main funds and complementary vehicles such as FSN Compass and the Confluence continuation programme, FSN has completed sixty platform deals and more than four hundred add-ons while maintaining close to thirty active portfolio companies; it has also committed to net-zero pathways with science-based targets.

portfolio_companies.name: Active Brands
portfolio_companies.description: Premium sports apparel and outdoor equipment brand house
portfolio_companies.detailed_description: Active Brands is a Norwegian house of premium outdoor sports apparel and equipment brands headquartered in Oslo. The company manages brands including Kari Traa, Dæhlie, Sweet Protection, Johaug, Bula, Åsnes, Vossatassar, and Pilago, serving customers across Europe, North America, and Asia. Founded in 2009, Active Brands has grown from a Norwegian brand incubation platform into a global brand accelerator with offices in Voss, Trysil, Broomfield, Shanghai, Gothenburg, Helsinki, Copenhagen, and Munich. The company achieved over NOK 1.35 billion in sales in 2021. In February 2022, FSN Capital (which first partnered with Active Brands in 2017) and Verdane agreed to co-invest, with FSN remaining as majority shareholder and Verdane becoming a significant minority shareholder to strengthen Active Brands' leadership in the outdoor industry and support international expansion and M&A.

portfolio_companies.name: Adragos Pharma
portfolio_companies.description: Contract development and manufacturing for pharmaceuticals
portfolio_companies.detailed_description: Adragos Pharma is a contract development and manufacturing organization (CDMO) specializing in small molecule, non-biotech pharmaceutical products, particularly injectables and semi-solid forms. The company operates production facilities in Livron (France), Leipzig (Germany), and Toride (Japan), employing over 400 people. In August 2021, FSN Capital VI acquired a majority stake, with the Prange Group retaining an investment to support continued growth. FSN's strategy focuses on add-on acquisitions in Europe and North America, particularly production sites divested by large pharmaceutical companies.

portfolio_companies.name: Bäcker Görtz
portfolio_companies.description: German bakery chain and QSR market leader
portfolio_companies.detailed_description: Bäcker Görtz is a leading German bakery chain founded in 1963 by the Görtz family, headquartered in Mannheim in the Rhine-Neckar region (neighboring Ludwigshafen across the Rhine). The company operates approximately 200 bakery branches with 1,400–1,900 employees, generating around €130 million in annual revenue. It produces over 2 million baked goods weekly from a highly automated production facility and achieved organic growth at 14% CAGR since 2006 with operating margins exceeding 20%. FSN Capital VI acquired a majority stake in September 2022 (closed October 2022); founders Peter and Frank Görtz retained a significant minority stake and continue as Co-CEOs. The investment targets acceleration in Germany's €15 billion bakery market through add-ons and operational best practices.

portfolio_companies.name: ilionx
portfolio_companies.description: Dutch IT services and digital transformation
portfolio_companies.detailed_description: ilionx is a leading Dutch IT-services provider founded in 2002 and headquartered in Utrecht. The company employs over 1,200 people across thirteen locations in the Netherlands, generating €205 million in revenue in 2022. It offers digital strategy, cloud-oriented application development, data & AI solutions, hyperautomation, and managed services primarily to regulated industries and the public sector. In December 2022, FSN Capital VI signed to acquire a majority stake (93% ownership); the transaction completed in early 2023. FSN's strategy focuses on organic expansion and add-on acquisitions in the fragmented Dutch IT-services market, building on experience with Netcompany, Fellowmind, Omegapoint, Nordlo, and Epista Life Science.

portfolio_companies.name: InstallatørGruppen
portfolio_companies.detailed_description: InstallatørGruppen is one of Denmark's largest companies in the technical installation sector, formed in February 2023 when FSN Capital VI partnered with 11 Danish installation companies: A-Comfort, Alvent Ventilation, Byens VVS & Blik, CH VVS, El Team Fyn, GL VVS, Jesper Hansen VVS, John Jensen VVS, Kjellerup Group, MH Elektric, and WeCon. The platform started with combined turnover of approximately DKK 1.3 billion and operates in electricity, plumbing, optical fiber, ventilation, and cooling. By end-2024 it had expanded to 32 companies across Denmark with roughly 1,590 professionals and an international office in Switzerland. The model is decentralized: each company retains local management, name, and location while benefiting from group scale and nationwide solutions.

portfolio_companies.name: Lobster
portfolio_companies.description: No-code data integration and process automation
portfolio_companies.detailed_description: Lobster is a German no-code data integration and digital process automation software provider, founded in 2002 and based in Tutzing. The company offers cloud-based tools for mid-to-large enterprises including Lobster_data (data and application integration), Lobster_pro (digital process automation), Lobster_pim (product information management), and logistics.cloud. It serves approximately 1,500 clients in logistics, commerce, and manufacturing with roughly 250 employees, and has delivered profitable revenue growth exceeding 20% annually since 2013. FSN Capital VI signed to acquire a majority stake in January 2023, supporting high-growth entrepreneurial businesses in connecting mission-critical IT systems.

portfolio_companies.name: Obton
portfolio_companies.detailed_description: Obton is a Danish solar energy investment company that develops, finances, and operates photovoltaic plants across Europe. The company specializes in large-scale solar park investments for institutional investors and utilities, managing the full lifecycle from development through construction and long-term operation.

portfolio_companies.name: Omegapoint
portfolio_companies.detailed_description: Omegapoint is one of Sweden's leading cybersecurity and secure digital transformation firms, founded in 2001 and headquartered in Stockholm with five offices across Sweden. The company specializes in secure software development, penetration testing, security assessments, and advisory services for blue-chip clients in banking, automotive, healthcare, and government. At acquisition it had LTM sales of SEK 515 million and EBITDA of SEK 76 million. FSN Capital acquired a majority stake from Priveq and management in May 2021; management retained 33%. Under Priveq (2017–2021), the company grew from SEK 300 million to over SEK 500 million in sales through six acquisitions and organic growth.

portfolio_companies.name: Optigroup
portfolio_companies.detailed_description: Optigroup is a leading Northern European B2B distributor formed by FSN Capital VI through the December 2021 combination of OptiGroup and Hygas, finalized in March 2022. The merged group has combined revenues of approximately €1.3 billion, EBITDA over €100 million, 1,900 employees, 70 distribution centers across 20 countries, and 100,000+ customers. OptiGroup focuses on Facility, Safety & Foodservice, Packaging Solutions, and Business Supplies; Hygas covers Facility, Safety & Hygiene, Medical, and Packaging in the Benelux. FSN became leading shareholder with full management control; Altor Fund II, Triton Fund II, and RoosGruppen retained minority stakes. The platform targets the €150 billion European B2B distribution market.

portfolio_companies.name: Polytech
portfolio_companies.description: Wind turbine blade protection and solutions
portfolio_companies.detailed_description: Polytech is a global leader in wind turbine rotor-blade technical solutions, founded in 1994 and headquartered in Bramming, Denmark. The company employs approximately 500 people across Denmark, China, Germany, and Mexico. Its solutions—including lightning protection, leading edge protection, sensor technology, transportation, and subsea cable protection—are used on more than 60% of all wind turbine blades produced globally. In January 2023, FSN Capital VI and Verdane agreed an investment of approximately €135 million; Verdane remained majority shareholder (50%), FSN took 40%, and management the remainder. The investment aims to accelerate Polytech's global leadership as wind turbines become larger and more complex.

portfolio_companies.name: Swash Group
portfolio_companies.detailed_description: Swash Group (formerly MEGABAD) is a leading German provider of bathroom solutions and sanitaryware, operating in the €15+ billion European bathroom market. The company offers a comprehensive range of bathroom products, design services, and installation solutions for residential and commercial customers. FSN Capital VI partnered with existing owners in July 2021 to support further growth and expansion in the fragmented bathroom sector.

portfolio_companies.name: Volue Infrastructure
portfolio_companies.detailed_description: Volue Infrastructure is a Norwegian software company specializing in mission-critical niche infrastructure software, carved out from Volue in 2025. Headquartered in Trondheim, it employs approximately 100 people and operates primarily under the Gemini brand. The software suite covers Water & Community (Portal+ asset management, Water Alert leakage detection, hydraulic modeling, document digitization) and Heavy Construction. FSN Capital VI signed to acquire the division in July 2025, with closing expected in Q4 2025. The investment targets growth from aging infrastructure investment, regulatory pressure, and digital adoption in Northern Europe.

portfolio_companies.name: BoldR
portfolio_companies.description: European market leader in trailer towing products
portfolio_companies.detailed_description: BoldR (formerly Rameder Group) is a European market leader in trailer towing products, founded in 1996 and headquartered in Leutenberg, Germany. The company distributes towbars, bike carriers, and roof racks across Europe, selling approximately 300,000 towbars yearly with annual revenue of around €70 million. It employs some 200 people, operates online shops in over ten countries, and maintains 16 assembly points in Germany for professional towbar installation. Prior to the acquisition, Rameder had strengthened its market position through strategic add-ons including Bertelshofer (2012), France Attelage (2017), and ELSA in the Czech Republic (2018). FSN Capital V signed the acquisition agreement in December 2018 and completed the deal in early 2019—FSN's first platform investment in Germany—acquiring a majority stake from Findos Investor; managing directors Dirk Schöler and Stefan Bertelshofer retained material stakes and continue to lead the business.

portfolio_companies.name: Ecovium
portfolio_companies.description: End-to-end logistics software and supply chain solutions
portfolio_companies.detailed_description: Ecovium is a leading provider of end-to-end logistics software solutions based in Neustadt am Rübenberge, Germany, serving the DACH region and over 30 countries. Its integrated software covers customs, warehouse management, shipping, transport management, and hardware solutions across the supply chain. Formally created in 2021 through the merger of MHP Solution Group companies, Ecovium recorded sales of €56 million in 2022. FSN Capital acquired a 74% stake in November 2020, targeting value through digitalization, market consolidation, commercial excellence, and cloud transition.

portfolio_companies.name: Fellowmind
portfolio_companies.description: Microsoft Dynamics and cloud solutions consulting
portfolio_companies.detailed_description: Fellowmind is a technology consulting firm specializing in Microsoft Dynamics, Power Platform, and cloud solutions across the Nordics and Europe. Headquartered in Stockholm, the company grew from around 1,000 employees at acquisition to 1,450 by September 2020, and has completed multiple add-on acquisitions in Poland and Germany. FSN Capital acquired a majority stake on 5 November 2019 as part of a three-company deal with eCraft (Finland) and Orango (Sweden), targeting the business software systems market.

portfolio_companies.name: Gram Equipment
portfolio_companies.description: Ice cream processing and freezing refrigeration equipment
portfolio_companies.detailed_description: Gram Equipment is a Danish company headquartered in Kolding that specializes in advanced ice cream processing and freezing refrigeration equipment for the food and beverage industry. FSN Capital V acquired the company in January 2018 from Swedish-based Procuritas Capital. The company designs, manufactures, and delivers processing lines, hardening tunnels, and related equipment for the global ice cream and frozen food markets.

portfolio_companies.name: Håndverksgruppen
portfolio_companies.detailed_description: Håndverksgruppen is the only pan-Norwegian integrated group within surface treatment services, operating in painting, flooring, and tiling. Originally established as a voluntary chain in 2012 with 12 companies, it grew to 43–44 member companies by 2019. In July 2020, FSN Capital V acquired majority stakes in 30–31 companies from Håndverksgruppen AS, obtaining approximately 77% ownership; the remainder stayed with management and founders who reinvested. The 31 companies generated approximately NOK 1,100 million in sales and NOK 100 million in EBITDA in 2019. The group serves corporate customers, municipalities, and building contractors across Norway.

portfolio_companies.name: Holmbergs Safety System
portfolio_companies.description: Mission-critical safety systems for child safety seats
portfolio_companies.detailed_description: Holmbergs Safety System is a Swedish company headquartered in Forserum that develops and manufactures mission-critical safety systems for the child safety seat industry. FSN Capital V acquired a majority stake in June 2017; existing management and owners reinvested alongside FSN and retained a material stake in the company.

portfolio_companies.name: IMPREG
portfolio_companies.description: Pipe liner systems and trenchless rehabilitation
portfolio_companies.detailed_description: IMPREG is a Danish manufacturer of pipe liner systems for trenchless rehabilitation of sewer, water, and industrial pipelines. Headquartered in Hellerup, the company provides cured-in-place pipe (CIPP) and related solutions for infrastructure maintenance. FSN Capital V signed an agreement to acquire IMPREG from BWB Partners in November 2019.

portfolio_companies.name: TASKING
portfolio_companies.description: Embedded compilers and tools for automotive and industrial
portfolio_companies.detailed_description: TASKING is a leading provider of embedded compilers, linkers, and debuggers for automotive and industrial applications, with emphasis on ADAS and autonomous driving. Its tools are used by over 50,000 engineers globally and power code in millions of vehicles. FSN Capital acquired 100% of TASKING from Altium in December 2020 for approximately $146 million, establishing TASKING as an independent software provider with FSN holding 91% and management the remainder. Headquartered in Munich, the company serves OEMs and Tier 1 suppliers.

portfolio_companies.name: ViaCon
portfolio_companies.description: Culverts, bridges, and civil engineering solutions
portfolio_companies.detailed_description: ViaCon is a Sweden-based provider of culverts, bridges, drainage systems, and civil engineering solutions. Originally part of the Saferoad Group when FSN Capital V acquired it in Q3 2018 (take-private at NOK 2 billion), ViaCon was operationally and financially separated and established as an independent company with its own management and strategy by 2021. Under FSN, ViaCon reorganized from 30+ legal entities into three core business areas and shifted to higher-margin engineered products; EBITDA grew from NOK 116 million in FY 2019 to NOK 243 million by September 2021. Headquartered in Lidköping.

portfolio_companies.name: Epista Life Science
portfolio_companies.description: Regulatory, quality, and technology services for life sciences
portfolio_companies.detailed_description: Epista Life Science provides regulatory affairs, quality management, and technology services to the pharmaceutical, biotech, and medical device industries across the Nordics and Europe. FSN Capital Compass I signed an agreement to acquire a majority stake on 25 May 2022. The company has expanded through add-on acquisitions such as Bruun & Maté to increase industry impact.

portfolio_companies.name: Firesafe
portfolio_companies.detailed_description: Firesafe is a leading Nordic fire safety services provider headquartered in Lorenskog, Norway, offering installation, maintenance, inspection, and advisory services for building fire protection across Norway, Sweden, Denmark, and Finland. The company operates as a one-stop-shop covering both active fire protection systems (deployed to directly fight fires) and passive fire protection systems (designed to prevent fire spread), plus fire technical consulting and specialized product sales. At acquisition it employed more than 800 people and generated net revenues exceeding NOK 1 billion in 2021; it has since grown to approximately NOK 1.4 billion turnover and over 1,000 employees, positioning Firesafe as the market leader in the Nordic fire safety sector. FSN Capital acquired a majority stake on 27 May 2022 from founder Morten Ameln, who had been sole shareholder since 2010; Ameln reinvested to retain a material minority stake. The partnership aims to drive organic expansion and add-on acquisitions across the fragmented Nordic fire protection market.

portfolio_companies.name: Seriline
portfolio_companies.detailed_description: Seriline is a Swedish provider of Identity and Access Management (IAM) solutions headquartered in Stockholm, founded in 1990. The company offers physical access management including physical credentials, card readers, and online identity management systems, serving large corporations, universities, hospitals, and public sector institutions. At acquisition it had 29 employees and SEK 84 million in sales (2021). FSN Capital acquired a majority stake on 18 February 2022, with founders Freddie and Pierre Parrman retaining a sizable stake. FSN intends to use Seriline as a platform for a buy-and-build strategy to create a Nordic cybersecurity leader within IAM.

portfolio_companies.name: Solcellespesialisten
portfolio_companies.description: Norway's largest supplier and installer of solar energy systems
portfolio_companies.detailed_description: Solcellespesialisten is Norway's largest supplier and installer of solar energy systems, founded in 2005 by current CEO Carl Christian Strømberg. The company has delivered over 4,000 solar systems and provides turnkey solutions across commercial, residential, and utility segments, with a complete value chain from sourcing through installation and aftermarket support. FSN Capital signed an agreement to acquire a majority stake in June 2023; existing shareholders remained as significant minority shareholders. The investment targets Norway's lagging solar penetration versus neighboring countries and growing demand for distributed energy resources.

portfolio_companies.name: Uhl Bau
portfolio_companies.description: Underground civil engineering for critical infrastructure
portfolio_companies.detailed_description: UHL Bau is a leading Austrian specialist in underground civil engineering for electricity, gas, water, sewage, district heating, and telecommunications networks. Founded by the Uhl family in 1966 and headquartered in Wiener Neustadt, the company generated revenues of over €80 million in 2024 and employs around 460 people, with profitable growth exceeding 10% annually over the last 15 years. The transaction includes UHL Elektro GmbH, a wholly owned subsidiary specializing in installation and energy technology. FSN Capital Compass I signed an agreement to invest in 2025—FSN's first platform investment in Austria. Jürgen and Sabine Uhl retain a significant minority stake. Closing expected Q3 2025 subject to regulatory approval.

portfolio_companies.name: Nordlo
portfolio_companies.detailed_description: Nordlo is a leading Nordic managed IT service provider originally established through a three-way merger facilitated by FSN Capital V in 2018 (Office-IT Partner, Zetup, Dicom). The company has completed 15 strategic add-on acquisitions and continues its growth trajectory. In August 2024, FSN Capital Confluence—a €588 million continuation fund with LGT Capital Partners as lead investor—acquired Nordlo from FSN Capital V to provide additional capital for organic growth and M&A. Nordlo completed a SEK 3.6 billion refinancing in 2024. Headquartered in Stockholm.

portfolio_companies.name: Saferoad
portfolio_companies.description: One of Europe's largest road safety and infrastructure solutions providers
portfolio_companies.detailed_description: Saferoad is one of Europe's largest providers of road safety and infrastructure solutions including barriers, signage, and related products. Originally acquired by FSN Capital V in Q3 2018 as part of a take-private of the Saferoad Group (NOK 2 billion) from Oslo Stock Exchange, ViaCon was separated in 2021 and Saferoad continued as the road safety division. Under FSN Capital V ownership (2018–2024), EBITDA nearly doubled. In 2024 the company reported underlying operating revenue of NOK 7,584 million and underlying EBITDA of NOK 820 million. In August 2024, FSN Capital Confluence acquired Saferoad to support a new phase of European M&A. Headquartered in Ørsta, Norway.

Fidelio Capital.name: Fidelio Capital
Fidelio Capital.description: Fidelio Capital is a Stockholm-based private equity firm partnering with Nordic companies. The team combines backgrounds from leading international investors, banks, and advisors with a hands-on approach to value creation and long-term ownership.

portfolio_companies.detailed_description: Backing the next phase of growth for a Northern European property house — advisory, transactions and capital markets across the Nordics and Baltics.

portfolio_companies.name: Alphalyse

portfolio_companies.detailed_description: Pioneering provider of host cell protein impurity testing and LC-MS-based analytics for biologic drugs; Danish specialist CRO supporting global biopharma.

portfolio_companies.name: KEYTO
portfolio_companies.detailed_description: A technology-enabled one-stop destination for home services — cleaning, white goods, gardening, handyman and related services across Sweden and the UK.

portfolio_companies.name: ibinder

portfolio_companies.detailed_description: Digital solutions for the construction and real estate industries — document management, collaboration and compliance for projects and assets.

portfolio_companies.name: Odevo

portfolio_companies.name: Vimian

portfolio_companies.detailed_description: Improving animal health for better lives — listed group spanning veterinary services, diagnostics, pharmaceuticals and technology (incl. segments such as AniCura).

portfolio_companies.name: Greenfood
portfolio_companies.detailed_description: Leading European healthy food provider — brands, distribution and concepts including salad bars and fresh categories; long-term Fidelio partnership, majority ownership from 2025.

portfolio_companies.name: AniCura

timeline.description: Majority in Danish CRO focused on LC-MS analytics for biologic drugs; supports global growth
Increases ownership to majority; partnership since 2012; takes over from Nordic Capital
Odevo enters Spain, Italy, Mexico, and Portugal as Portik joins the group
Agreement to sell Fresh Produce business to Dole Nordic AB
Alireza Tajbakhsh appointed Chief Executive Officer
Strategic additions, new service vertical, and expansion across Sweden and the UK

company_updates.title: KEYTO expands with two strategic additions and launches new service vertical
company_updates.text: Strengthens its one-stop home services platform in Sweden; broadens offering and establishes a new growth vertical.

company_updates.title: KEYTO accelerates further through three strategic expansions
company_updates.text: Late 2025 initiatives strengthen the service offering across Sweden and the UK; reinforces customer value and trusted service brands.

company_updates.title: Greenfood signs agreement to sell its Fresh Produce business
company_updates.text: Agreement with Dole Nordic AB regarding the sale of Greenfood’s Fresh Produce business.

company_updates.title: KEYTO makes two strategic investments
company_updates.text: Expands within Servly and gardening services in Sweden; strengthens the one-stop household services destination.

company_updates.title: Vimian appoints Alireza Tajbakhsh as Chief Executive Officer
company_updates.text: Joined Vimian in 2021; previously Head of Veterinary Services and interim Head of MedTech.

company_updates.title: Greenfood expands in France
company_updates.text: Picadeli signs a three-year agreement with TotalEnergies to expand at motorway service stations in France.

company_updates.title: Fidelio increases ownership in Greenfood
company_updates.text: Fidelio becomes majority owner, taking over from Nordic Capital; partnership dating to 2012.

company_updates.title: Fidelio invests in Alphalyse to support global growth in biologic drug analytics
company_updates.text: Majority stake in Danish CRO focused on liquid-chromatography mass-spectrometry (LC-MS) based analytics.

company_updates.title: Odevo expands to southern Europe and Latin America as Portik joins the group
company_updates.text: Entry into Spain, Italy, Mexico, and Portugal; Portik adds scale in fragmented property-management markets.
